ops.text: authkit/locate_test.go: TestRequireHelperFailsClosedWhenMissing + TestHelperAppPathSkipsBundleWithoutExecutable are non-hermetic and leak into the real Caskroom. brewPrefixes() (authkit/locate.go:32-38) hardcodes /opt/homebrew + /usr/local with no env override, so the 'expect HelperError when absent' tests find the real staged authkit cask (/opt/homebrew/Caskroom/authkit/0.2.0/...) on a dev box that has it installed, and pass-when-should-fail-closed. Pre-existing (files unmodified in git), passes on CI / any machine without the cask, and OUT of the Track S rewire blast radius (U2-U8 touch rpc/hostregistry/proc/drain/version/service, not authkit). A hermetic fix needs a production test seam in brewPrefixes() — but that is security-sensitive fail-closed helper discovery, so it wants its own scoped change + review, not a Track S drive-by. Follow-up for an authkit-owner pass.
